MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Adeena Brown Faison appeared by zoom in propria persona.

This was a pre-granted matter. Mrs. Faison stated that she is objecting to the payment of the property maintenance. The Second and Final Report and First and Final Account of Administrator, etc., is continued to May 20, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mrs. Faison is to file her objections in writing on or before April 29, 2024. A response is to be filed by May 13, 2024. The court informed Mrs. Faison that if her objections are not filed by the deadline, the court may not accept her objections.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

Off Calendar Dec-14-2020 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Feb-24-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christopher Jew appeared by zoom for and with Crystal Williams (Petitioner) by zoom.
Merlin Williams appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.

The Court noted that this matter was continued once based on Mr. Williams appearance to object, that Mr. Williams was told to file written objections and that nothing has been filed. Mr. Williams stated that he has not filed anything and that he can't afford to retain an Attorney. The Court stated that notice is proper, that Mr. Williams was allowed an opportunity for objection and that it has not received anything. The Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration. Crystal Williams is appointed as the Administrator the estate with no authority as none was requested. Bond is set at $20,000.00. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for June 8, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Jan-27-2021 Continued To Feb-24-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christopher Jew appeared by zoom for and with Crystal Williams by zoom.
Merlin Williams appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.
Adeena Brown appeared by zoom in Propria Persona.

The Court that the petition was previously on calendar, that there was proper notice, and that is prepared to grant the petition without a hearing, but Mr. Williams appeared to object. Mr. Williams stated that he is trying to meet with the seven children and that he needs a little more time to figure out who will fairly administer the estate. Mr. Jew stated that this is a blended family, that Mr. Tubutu and his Wife (previously deceased) had children from another relationship, provided history and stated that there was a petition for determination of persons entitled to distribution and Adeena was removed as the Administrator. Mr. Jew also stated that there are three blood related siblings and four that are related to the pre-deceased spouse, that he filed his petition for the appointment of his Client on November 16, 2020, that there is a piece of real property that nothing is being attended to, that there is no current Administrator and that Adeena was suspended in July of 2020. The Court stated that it is continuing this one more time for written objections or filing of a competing petition, and that this needs to be teed up by the next hearing or the Court is moving forward. Mr. Jew stated that this has been going on for quite some time, that notice went out in December, that the property is not being attended to and that nothing has been done. The Court stated that it sees no urgency, that this is the second calling of the case, that there will be no more than three callings, that it needs to move this forward and that it will make a decision based on the information that is before it. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to February 24, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Any written objections or competing petitions are to be filed and served by February 17, 2021, and courtesy copies are to be provided to the Court.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Off Calendar Oct-09-2020 Continued To Nov-10-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel - Civil
Off Calendar Sep-17-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Rueppel appeared by zoom for Adeena Brown (Former Administrator).

The Court noted that the Department of Health Care Services has a request for special notice on file and that they were not served with Mr. Rueppel's motion. Mr. Rueppel stated that he may not have served them and that he does believe the Department of Health Care Services has made an appearance. The Court stated that it reads the code to say that service of this motion is to provide notice on all parties who have appeared, that it would have preferred that notice had been given to the Department of health Care Services, and noted this issue has been percolating in this matter. Mr. Rueppel stated that he has had no contact from Ms. Brown for quite some time, and that he has tried to contact her, but he has not been able to reach her. The Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel is under submission with the Court's intent to grant the motion.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Aug-25-2020 Off Calendar

Petition For Determination Of Persons Entitled To Distribution; To Set Aside Order For Probate Based On Extrinsic Fraud And For Appointment Of Special Administrator
Off Calendar Jul-28-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christopher Jew appeared by telephone for Crystal Williams (Petitioner).
Adam Lazar appeared by zoom for Adeena Brown (Administrator).

Mr. Lazar stated that Mr. Rueppel filed a motion to be relieved as Counsel with a hearing date of September 8, 2020, that they have been unable to communicate with the Administrator and requested a continuance until the motion is heard. The Court stated that it will not continue the matter, that it is not inclined to grant all of the relief that is being requested and that it wants to make sure that the estate moves forward. The Court also stated that any rights can be claimed at a later time, that it is going to set aside the appointment order and deny the request for a special administrator, and that the rest of this petition is off calendar. In addition, the Court stated that it is not prejudicing any ex parte petitions and that it will not rule on determination of heirship. The Petition for Determination of Persons Entitled to Distribution, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Cabasares. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION

Off Calendar Mar-25-2020 Continued To Jul-21-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Determination Of Persons Entitled To Distribution; To Set Aside Order For Probate Based On Extrinsic Fraud And For Appointment Of Special Administrator
Off Calendar Mar-24-2020 Continued To Apr-28-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Determination Of Persons Entitled To Distribution; To Set Aside Order For Probate Based On Extrinsic Fraud And For Appointment Of Special Administrator
Off Calendar Feb-25-2020 Continued To Mar-24-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christopher Jew for and with Crystal Williams (Petitioner).
John Rueppel for Adeena Brown (Personal Representative).

Mr. Rueppel stated that the DNA results were inconclusive as to paternity given the sample they had to compare to is not a close enough line, that the current plan is to again try and get the sealed birth certificate and that the Family Law petition is prepared. Mr. Jew stated that it is four months later and they are back at the same place they were in November, that he does not see a reason to prolong this matter, that this issue was brought in June of last year and raised the issue of the impact it has on the civil suit that was brought against the Administrator. Mr. Rueppel stated that his Client does not have a copy of the sealed birth certificate, that it his understanding that his Client saw one a couple of decades ago and that his Client has gone to the Records Department, but can't see it without a Court order. The Court stated that it will allow one final continuance. The Petition for Determination of Persons Entitled to Distribution, etc. is continued to March 24, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Rueppel provided courtesy copies of the opposition.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

Petition For Determination Of Persons Entitled To Distribution; To Set Aside Order For Probate Based On Extrinsic Fraud And For Appointment Of Special Administrator
Off Calendar Jan-14-2020 Continued To Feb-25-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christopher Jew for and with Crystal Williams.
John Rueppel for Adeena Brown (Personal Representative).

Mr. Rueppel stated that they haven't yet been able to obtain the sealed birth certificate, that his Client did a DNA test and expects to have the results back at any time now to substantiate the relationship, and asked for setting of an evidentiary hearing on the issue of parentage. Mr. Jew stated that this issue has been up since June, that it is unfair to his Client to continue to wait on this issue, that the original Heirs have not appeared, and that it is the Administrator's responsibility to remain neutral and not take one side over the other. Mr. Rueppel stated that the DNA test was performed on early to mid-December and that he does not think an evidentiary will take long. Mr. Jew stated that this is the first he has heard of the DNA test and that he wants to do some discovery. The Petition for Determination of Persons Entitled to Distribution, etc. is continued to February 25, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court expects Mr. Rueppel to provide the DNA test results to Mr. Jew as soon as he gets them. A status statement is to be filed by February 18, 2020.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

Petition For Determination Of Persons Entitled To Distribution; To Set Aside Order For Probate Based On Extrinsic Fraud And For Appointment Of Special Administrator
Off Calendar Dec-17-2019 Continued To Jan-14-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Determination Of Persons Entitled To Distribution; To Set Aside Order For Probate Based On Extrinsic Fraud And For Appointment Of Special Administrator
Off Calendar Nov-12-2019 Continued To Dec-17-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christopher Jew for and with Crystal Williams (Petitioner).
John Rueppel for Adeena Brown (Administrator).
Amy Williams also present.

Mr. Jew stated that they spoke this morning, that Mr. Rueppel's Client believes there is a sealed birth certificate, but is having difficulty obtaining it and that his Client (Crystal) was able to request an informational birth certificate. Mr. Rueppel stated that the birth certificate was sealed for reasons that are not entirely clear, that they discussed two procedures used for unsealing a birth certificate and that he will be filing a motion in Family Law to have the Court unseal the birth certificate and to see Adeena's relationship. Mr. Rueppel also informed the Court that there is a Civil dispute alleging elder abuse. The Petition for Determination of Persons Entitled to Distribution, etc. is continued to December 17, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court expects that there will be good faith and high priority in unraveling this.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.
